title: Wild Oasis Website Planning

learningObjectives.text: Explain routes and layouts using a new example and justify the result.
Explain feature planning using a new example and justify the result.
Choose and justify server/client responsibility planning using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: Wild Oasis Website Planning is part of the mental model learners need before making reliable implementation decisions. It belongs to the **Project Planning** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Routes and layouts** → **Feature planning** → **Server/client responsibility planning**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: Routes and layouts
sections.body: Plan routes around tasks and resources, using nested layouts for persistent shared UI and route-level loading or error boundaries.

sections.title: Feature planning
sections.body: Translate user-visible outcomes into routes, data needs, interactions, errors, and acceptance conditions before implementation.

sections.title: Server/client responsibility planning
sections.body: Default to Server Components for data and noninteractive UI, then add narrow Client Components at interaction boundaries.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.source:
app/
  tracks/
    [track]/
      layout.tsx
      page.tsx
      [module]/
        page.tsx
        loading.tsx
        error.tsx
sections.explanation: The route tree exposes URLs, shared layouts, and loading/error boundaries before coding.

commonMistakes: Marking a large route tree as client code when only a small interaction needs it.
Confusing React Server Components with server-side rendering.
Assuming cached data is fresh without an explicit policy and invalidation path.
Placing security-sensitive validation only in the client or middleware layer.
summary: Wild Oasis Website Planning connects Routes and layouts, Feature planning, Server/client responsibility planning.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

questions.prompt: Which statement best explains “Routes and layouts” in the context of Wild Oasis Website Planning?

choices.label: Client Components can submit to or invoke a Server Action while the function itself executes on the server.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Routes and layouts.

choices.label: Dynamic rendering produces output at request time for request-specific, personalized, or uncached data.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Routes and layouts.

choices.label: Plan routes around tasks and resources, using nested layouts for persistent shared UI and route-level loading or error boundaries.
choices.feedback: Routes and layouts is best understood as follows: Plan routes around tasks and resources, using nested layouts for persistent shared UI and route-level loading or error boundaries.

choices.label: Hydration connects React behavior to matching server-rendered HTML. The first client render must agree with server output.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Routes and layouts.

questions.explanation: Routes and layouts is best understood as follows: Plan routes around tasks and resources, using nested layouts for persistent shared UI and route-level loading or error boundaries.
questions.hint: Focus on what the concept changes, enables, or constrains—not only on its syntax.

questions.prompt: A learner must use Wild Oasis Website Planning in a new situation. Which approach best demonstrates transferable understanding?

choices.label: Apply Routes and layouts deliberately, make the relevant inputs and assumptions explicit, and verify the observable result before adding more complexity.
choices.feedback: Transfer comes from using the concept with explicit assumptions and checking the resulting behavior, not from reproducing a memorized example.

choices.label: Skip the underlying model and rely on memorized syntax, even when the result is unclear.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Wild Oasis Website Planning.

choices.label: Treat the concept as isolated trivia and avoid connecting it to earlier prerequisites.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Wild Oasis Website Planning.

choices.label: Add more state and abstraction immediately, before identifying the smallest requirement.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Wild Oasis Website Planning.

questions.explanation: Transfer comes from using the concept with explicit assumptions and checking the resulting behavior, not from reproducing a memorized example.
questions.hint: Choose the option that would still work when the surrounding example changes.

questions.prompt: Which response best addresses this common mistake: “Marking a large route tree as client code when only a small interaction needs it.”?

choices.label: The statement is a misconception. A better approach is to reason from the concept's inputs, rules, and observable outcome, then verify the result.
choices.feedback: Good feedback names the misconception and replaces it with a usable reasoning process.

choices.label: The statement is unrelated to program behavior and cannot cause defects.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Wild Oasis Website Planning.

choices.label: The statement is always correct, so no further reasoning about Wild Oasis Website Planning is needed.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Wild Oasis Website Planning.

choices.label: The statement is correct only when variable names are short.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Wild Oasis Website Planning.

questions.explanation: Good feedback names the misconception and replaces it with a usable reasoning process.
questions.hint: Look for the option that corrects both the belief and the learner's next action.

questions.prompt: Which sequence is most reliable when solving a problem involving Wild Oasis Website Planning?

choices.label: Optimize first, then decide what the code is supposed to do.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Wild Oasis Website Planning.

choices.label: First identify the requirement, then apply the relevant rule from Routes and layouts, inspect the result, and only then refactor or optimize.
choices.feedback: The reliable sequence starts from the requirement, applies a known rule, observes behavior, and improves the solution afterward.

choices.label: Start with a framework abstraction and avoid checking the underlying language behavior.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Wild Oasis Website Planning.

choices.label: Copy an unrelated implementation, hide the assumptions, and test only the happy path.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Wild Oasis Website Planning.

questions.explanation: The reliable sequence starts from the requirement, applies a known rule, observes behavior, and improves the solution afterward.
questions.hint: Prefer a sequence that makes failures visible early.

questions.prompt: Which guideline shows the best judgment about when to use Wild Oasis Website Planning?

choices.label: Avoid Wild Oasis Website Planning completely because all abstractions reduce maintainability.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Wild Oasis Website Planning.

choices.label: Choose based only on line count, without considering readability, correctness, or future change.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Wild Oasis Website Planning.

choices.label: Use Wild Oasis Website Planning when it makes intent, ownership, or data flow clearer; avoid adding it mechanically when a simpler, equally explicit solution is sufficient.
choices.feedback: Engineering judgment balances clarity and cost. The concept should serve the requirement rather than become an end in itself.

choices.label: Use Wild Oasis Website Planning in every file because more abstraction is always better.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Wild Oasis Website Planning.

questions.explanation: Engineering judgment balances clarity and cost. The concept should serve the requirement rather than become an end in itself.
questions.hint: Choose the option that acknowledges both benefits and trade-offs.
